fire_rating.ul_design: UL D916 (or equivalent restrained beam assembly)

components.name: W-Beam (Wide Flange)
components.description: Wide flange beam — web and flanges form I-profile

components.name: SFRM Spray Coat — 4-Sided Application
components.description: Spray-applied fireproofing encapsulating all four sides of the beam (top flange top, bottom flange bottom, both flange edges, and web faces)

components.name: Steel Primer (if required)
components.description: Primer applied to steel substrate when required for adhesion or corrosion resistance. Required when steel has been painted or has mill scale issues.

properties.compatibility_note: Must be listed as compatible by SFRM manufacturer

components.name: Mesh Reinforcement at Corners
components.material: Galvanized welded wire mesh or expanded metal lath
components.description: Reinforcing mesh applied at flange tips and re-entrant corners to prevent SFRM cracking and delamination at geometric transitions

properties.mesh_gauge: 20 ga expanded metal lath or 1-inch hex mesh
properties.attachment: Mechanical fastener or speed clip to steel
properties.coverage: Wrap around flange tips min 2 inches each side

assembly_constraints.description: Minimum SFRM thickness per UL design listing based on beam Hp/A (W/D) ratio
assembly_constraints.rule: SFRM thickness shall not be less than the value specified in the UL design listing for the specific W/D ratio of the beam

assembly_constraints.description: Steel surfaces must be clean, free of oil, grease, loose mill scale, and dirt prior to SFRM application
assembly_constraints.rule: Surface prep per AWCI 12-B and SFRM manufacturer requirements

assembly_constraints.description: Ambient and substrate temperature must be minimum 40 deg F during application and curing
Mesh reinforcement required at all flange tip corners where SFRM thickness exceeds 1 inch
assembly_constraints.rule: Install mesh at all re-entrant corners and flange edges per manufacturer detail

assembly_constraints.description: Bond strength adhesion/cohesion testing per ASTM E736
assembly_constraints.rule: Min 1 test per 10,000 sq ft or per floor, whichever is more frequent
